preview.summary: Steve Clarke's Scotland face a star-studded Brazil under Carlo Ancelotti in a Group C heavyweight test. The Scots will sit deep, defend in numbers and look to spring McTominay on the break, while Brazil's attacking talent demands sustained pressure. A defining group-stage fixture for Scotland's knockout hopes.
preview.homeForm: Scotland reached the finals via Europe but lost talisman Billy Gilmour to a late injury before the tournament.
preview.awayForm: Brazil are ranked sixth in the world and managed by Carlo Ancelotti with a deep attacking pool.
preview.homeKey: Scott McTominay, Napoli midfielder and Scotland's goal threat.
preview.awayKey: Vinicius Junior, electric winger leading Brazil's attack.
storylines: Ancelotti's Brazil bring Vinicius, Raphinha and Neymar to Miami
Scotland missing injured playmaker Billy Gilmour
A rematch of group-stage history from France 1998
preview.h2h: Brazil have won every prior meeting, including 1-0 in 1990 and 2-1 in the 1998 World Cup opener.
